caveats: single run per arm (survivor count high-variance)
truncation was already half-mitigated by the existing compression-hint retry
savings direction (cap narrowing, reasoning routing) deferred with the reference arm
design: starved conjecturer cap (1200) on republic skeletons; controller OFF vs ON+liveness, 6 cycles each
verdict_does_it_work: YES on process metrics: the controller detected truncation from log signals and widened cap:conjecturer 1200->1920->3072, cutting truncation 0.40->0.17 and lifting valid-JSON 0.71->0.86, with 3 replayable policies carrying their evidence and zero constitution violations.
verdict_is_it_efficient: NO net savings at this scale: near-identical tokens (68.9k vs 67.8k, +1.6%). This is a recovery/correctness mechanism, not a savings one, as pre-stated. Survivors 2 vs 3 is within noise and even slightly lower — because the harness ALREADY has a truncation-aware compression retry that partially rescues the same failure by hand. The controller's marginal value is small HERE precisely because this failure was already patched once; its real value is auto-handling UNpatched failures.
